title: storage — declared entitlements
description: Three entitlements the storage module consumes. Two pool kinds (bytes + monthly uploads) + one slot kind (max file count per tenant). Overquota rejects at the middleware layer with HTTP 402.

entitlements.description: Total bytes currently referenced by non-erased File rows. Reconciled hourly via SyncStorageEntitlementUsageJob (SUM of File.size_bytes WHERE deleted_at IS NULL AND archived_at IS NULL). Reset window is `anniversary` because storage is a persistent resource — the cap represents the tenant's steady-state ceiling, not a monthly recurring budget.

enterprise.note: Unlimited by default; negotiated per contract

entitlements.description: Hard cap on File row count. Defence against pathological upload floods (a malicious script creating millions of tiny files to blow through DB row limits before hitting the byte cap).

Monthly upload count cap. Distinct from the file count slot — this is a THROUGHPUT limit that resets each calendar month, defending against sustained-abuse patterns where uploads are also being deleted in a cycle. Free tier: 500 uploads/month; enterprise: unlimited.

reservation_semantics.chunked_uploads: On ChunkedUpload initiate, the entire total_size_bytes is RESERVED against storage.bytes.consumed. Reservation held for the upload's TTL (24h default). On successful finalize, reservation flips to actual usage on the resulting File. On abort / expire, reservation is released via SyncStorageEntitlementUsageJob.
reservation_semantics.dedup_effect: When a dedup-hit occurs (matching SHA-256 already in the tenant), the second tenant still consumes bytes for its reference (each row 'pays' for the reference). Cross-tenant dedup applies to physical byte storage only — not to per-tenant accounting.
reservation_semantics.atomicity: Reservation + upload commit are atomic within the DB transaction. On roll-back (validation failure, virus detection during upload), the reservation is released via the FileObserver deleting hook.
